description: Fix the post-microservice-split RAG project so the full pipeline (register → login → KB → upload → process → chat with citations) runs end-to-end across the Java services (user/document/knowledge/gateway on Spring Cloud + Nacos) and the Python rag-ai-service (FastAPI). Infrastructure (MySQL/Nacos/ChromaDB/MinIO) is already up via docker compose.

userStories.title: Add missing Python deps to rag-ai-service/requirements.txt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want rag-ai-service to install cleanly so the FastAPI process can import all modules at startup.
acceptanceCriteria: rag-ai-service/requirements.txt contains: loguru, pydantic-settings, nacos-sdk-python (>=2.0.0, the v2.nacos API), httpx, pyyaml
In a clean venv: `pip install -r rag-ai-service/requirements.txt` succeeds
From rag-ai-service/: `python -c "import app.main"` exits 0 with no ImportError
Build/Typecheck passes (python -c "import app.main" returns 0)
userStories.notes: 切到 uv 管理：新建 pyproject.toml + uv.lock；requirements.txt 改由 uv export 生成（保留给 Docker 兼容）。Dockerfile / Dockerfile.dev 同步切到 uv sync --frozen。verified: uv run python -c "import app.main" 退出码 0；v2.nacos 命名空间可用（nacos-sdk-python 3.2.0）。

userStories.title: Add rag-ai-service Nacos config and local env defaults
userStories.description: As an operator, I want rag-ai-service to load its own Nacos config and run locally against host-mapped infra so it stops reading rag-gateway.yaml by mistake.
acceptanceCriteria: Create nacos-config/rag-ai-service.yaml with at least: server.port=8000, mysql (host=127.0.0.1, port=3306, db=ragwebui, user=ragwebui, password=ragwebui), minio (endpoint=127.0.0.1:9000, access/secret=minioadmin, bucket=documents), chromadb (host=127.0.0.1, port=8001)
Update rag-ai-service/app/core/config.py default NACOS_DATA_ID from 'rag-gateway.yaml' to 'rag-ai-service.yaml'
Add rag-ai-service/.env.example with MYSQL_SERVER=127.0.0.1, CHROMA_DB_HOST=127.0.0.1, CHROMA_DB_PORT=8001, MINIO_ENDPOINT=127.0.0.1:9000, NACOS_SERVER_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8848
Manual verification step in commit message: `python app/main.py` starts and `rag-ai-service` is visible in Nacos http://127.0.0.1:8848/nacos service list
Build/Typecheck passes (python -c "from app.core.config import settings; assert settings.NACOS_DATA_ID == 'rag-ai-service.yaml'")
userStories.notes: nacos-config/ 在 .gitignore 中被整目录忽略（配置入 Nacos 后仅作本地参考），故 rag-ai-service.yaml 未进 git；config.py 同步把 NACOS_SERVER_ADDR 默认值从 localhost:8848 改为 127.0.0.1:8848，env_file 用 _SERVICE_ROOT 绝对路径锁定，避免 cwd 漂移导致读不到 .env。verified: uv run python -c 断言 NACOS_DATA_ID=='rag-ai-service.yaml' 退出码 0。

userStories.title: Wire LLM/Embedding API keys on Python and Java sides
userStories.description: As an operator, I want valid embedding/LLM credentials on both sides so the document pipeline and chat actually call the model.
acceptanceCriteria: rag-ai-service/.env.example documents OPENAI_API_KEY (and optional OPENAI_API_BASE / OPENAI_MODEL / OPENAI_EMBEDDINGS_MODEL / DASH_SCOPE_API_KEY / OLLAMA_API_BASE)
nacos-config/rag-document-service.yaml replaces 'your-openai-api-key-here' with a config placeholder pulled from environment variable (e.g. ${OPENAI_API_KEY:}) so secrets are not committed literally
rag-document-service/src/main/java/com/chuhezhe/ragdocumentservice/config/AIConfig.java fails fast with a clear error message when api-key is blank, instead of silently constructing a broken bean
Build/Typecheck passes (cd rag-document-service && ./mvnw -DskipTests package)
userStories.notes: .env.example 在 US-002 已经铺好 OPENAI_* / DASH_SCOPE_* / OLLAMA_* 占位；本迭代：nacos-config/rag-document-service.yaml 里 api-key 改成 ${OPENAI_API_KEY:}（Spring 占位符），AIConfig 在 bean 初始化时用 StringUtils.hasText 做 fail-fast（抛 IllegalStateException + 清晰提示）。顺手修复 pom.xml 缺 spring-boot-starter-test 导致 `./mvnw -DskipTests package` 在 testCompile 阶段失败的历史坑，否则后续 US-005/006/011 的 PRD build 命令也跑不过。verified: ./mvnw -DskipTests package 退出码 0。

userStories.title: Initialize ragwebui database tables from a single source of truth
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a deterministic way to create all 7 application tables so Java services do not crash on startup waiting for Python alembic.
acceptanceCriteria: Document the chosen approach in scripts/ralph/progress.txt: either (a) startup-order: rag-ai-service must boot first to run alembic upgrade head, or (b) extract DDL into .dockervolumes/mysql/sql/01_schema.sql so MySQL initializes the schema on first boot

If approach (b) is chosen: produce 01_schema.sql covering users, knowledge_bases, documents, document_uploads, processing_tasks, document_chunks, api_keys, chats, chat_knowledge_bases — generated from the alembic head schema
Manual verification step in commit message: `docker exec rag-service-db-1 mysql -uroot -proot -e "show tables from ragwebui"` lists all 9 tables
Build/Typecheck passes (sql file parses with `mysql --help` style sanity check, or `python -c "import alembic"` for approach (a))
userStories.notes: 选 approach (b)：.dockervolumes/mysql/sql/01_schema.sql，由 alembic head 3580c0dcd005 的 upgrade 串编译而成，覆盖 PRD 列出的 9 张表 + messages（alembic head 含但 PRD 漏列）+ alembic_version 种入 '3580c0dcd005'，后续 `alembic upgrade head` 为 no-op。已在临时库 dryrun 导入通过并在 ragwebui 直接 apply，`show tables from ragwebui` 返回 11 行（10 业务 + alembic_version），覆盖 PRD 9 张表；documents.file_size 暂时保留 INT（与 alembic head 一致），留给 US-008 改 BIGINT 并同步 migration + 01_schema.sql。注意：docker-entrypoint-initdb.d 只在 mysql 首次 init 时跑，.dockervolumes/mysql/data 已存在时不会触发；重装验证需先清 data 目录。

userStories.title: Fix KBClient feign path mismatch (404 on /api/ai/documents/process)
userStories.description: As the document service, I want my call to rag-knowledge-service to hit the real controller path so document processing can read knowledge-base info.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit rag-document-service/src/main/java/com/chuhezhe/ragdocumentservice/feign/KBClient.java
Change @GetMapping("/{kb_id}") to @GetMapping("/knowledge-base/{kb_id}") to match KnowledgeBaseController.@RequestMapping("/knowledge-base")
Manual verification step in commit message: POST /api/ai/documents/process returns 200/202 (no longer 404 from KBClient)
Build/Typecheck passes (cd rag-document-service && ./mvnw -DskipTests package)
userStories.notes: @GetMapping("/{kb_id}") → @GetMapping("/knowledge-base/{kb_id}")，与 rag-knowledge-service KnowledgeBaseController 的 @RequestMapping("/knowledge-base") + @GetMapping("/{kb_id}") 拼出的完整路径一致。verified: ./mvnw -DskipTests package 退出码 0；端到端 404 → 200 验证留给 US-013 smoke 脚本。

userStories.title: Reconcile /api/ai/documents/upload/record endpoint with KB upload flow
userStories.description: As the knowledge service, I want my Feign call for upload records to hit a real endpoint so PUT /knowledge-base/{id}/documents/upload does not 404 mid-flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Pick option A or B and document the choice in commit body
Option A: rag-document-service/.../controller/DocumentController.java adds @PostMapping("/upload/record") that delegates to documentUploadService.addUploadRecord and returns Result<Integer>

Option B: rag-knowledge-service/.../service/KnowledgeBaseService.uploadKnowledgeBaseDocuments stops calling addUploadDocumentRecord and relies solely on /api/ai/documents/upload (which already creates the upload record); also remove the now-dead method from rag-knowledge-service/.../feign/DocumentServiceClient.java
Manual verification: PUT /knowledge-base/{kb_id}/documents/upload returns success and SELECT * FROM document_uploads shows a new row
Build/Typecheck passes (cd rag-document-service && ./mvnw -DskipTests package && cd ../rag-knowledge-service && ./mvnw -DskipTests package)
userStories.notes: 选 Option B：/api/ai/documents/upload 已经在落 MinIO 的同一流程里调 documentUploadService.addUploadRecord 并把主键回写到 UploadDocResult.uploadDocRecordId；Option A 再开一个端点会产生重复 document_uploads 记录。改动：KnowledgeBaseService.uploadKnowledgeBaseDocuments 读 uploadRes.getData().getUploadDocRecordId()；删 DocumentServiceClient.addUploadDocumentRecord + 相关 UploadDocRecordDTO/BigInteger import；顺手把 rag-knowledge-service/pom.xml 也补上 spring-boot-starter-test（与 US-003 对 rag-document-service 做的修复一致）。verified: 两边 ./mvnw -DskipTests package 均退出 0；document_uploads 新增行的活体校验留给 US-013。

userStories.title: Remove duplicate KB CRUD/upload/process from rag-ai-service (Python)
userStories.description: As the architect, I want only Java rag-knowledge-service / rag-document-service to own KB CRUD and document ingestion so the Python side is solely responsible for retrieval, chat, openapi, api-keys.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete from rag-ai-service/app/api/api_v1/knowledge_base.py: POST '', GET '', GET '/{kb_id}', PUT '/{kb_id}', DELETE '/{kb_id}', POST '/{kb_id}/documents/upload', POST '/{kb_id}/documents/preview', POST '/{kb_id}/documents/process', GET '/{kb_id}/documents/tasks', GET '/{kb_id}/documents/{doc_id}', POST '/cleanup'
Keep POST '/test-retrieval' and any internal helper used by chat
Edit nacos-config/rag-gateway.yaml: remove route 'rag-ai-service-knowledge-base' (Path=/api/ai/knowledge-base/**); leave the Java route '/knowledge-base/**' as the single KB entrypoint
Manual verification: GET /api/ai/knowledge-base via gateway returns 404; GET /knowledge-base via gateway returns the user's KB list

Build/Typecheck passes (python -c "from app.api.api_v1 import knowledge_base" succeeds; python -c "from app.api.api_v1.knowledge_base import router; assert all(r.path != '/' for r in router.routes if r.methods == {'GET'})")
userStories.notes: knowledge_base.py 整文件重写，只保留 POST /test-retrieval（chat 检索调用要用）和必要 import；原 add_processing_tasks_to_queue 等辅助函数跟着被删的 endpoint 一起清掉（无跨文件引用）。nacos-config/rag-gateway.yaml 删掉 rag-ai-service-knowledge-base 路由，加注释解释 Java route 是 KB 单一入口。test-retrieval 仍挂在 /api/ai/knowledge-base/test-retrieval（因为 api.py 里 prefix 还是 /knowledge-base），但网关只保留 Java 路由 /knowledge-base/**，所以外部走网关只能打到 Java；chat 服务在 rag-ai-service 进程内直接调用 router 不受影响。verified: `uv run python -c 'from app.api.api_v1 import knowledge_base'` 退出 0；`assert all(r.path != '/' for r in router.routes if r.methods == {'GET'})` 通过（唯一 route 是 POST /test-retrieval）；`uv run python -c 'import app.main'` 退出 0。

userStories.title: Align Python ORM and Java MyBatis entity column types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want consistent column types across both sides so writes do not overflow and reads do not fail deserialization.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit 7 tables: users, knowledge_bases, documents, document_uploads, processing_tasks, document_chunks, api_keys
Confirm/fix documents.file_size as BIGINT to match Java Document.fileSize (BigInteger); update rag-ai-service/app/models/knowledge.py and add an alembic migration if a column type change is needed
Produce scripts/ralph/check_schema.sql: a script that SELECTs information_schema.COLUMNS for those 7 tables and prints column_name + data_type + is_nullable
Add scripts/ralph/expected_schema.md: a table per entity comparing Python ORM column, Java @TableField, and MySQL actual
Manual verification: running `mysql -uroot -proot ragwebui < scripts/ralph/check_schema.sql` matches expected_schema.md with zero diffs
Build/Typecheck passes (cd rag-document-service && ./mvnw -DskipTests package && python -c "from app.models.knowledge import Document")
userStories.notes: documents.file_size: Python ORM 已经是 BigInteger NOT NULL，Java Document.fileSize 也是 BigInteger，只有 MySQL 从 alembic initial 继承的 INT NULL 对不上。补上 alembic migration a1b2c3d4e5f6_align_documents_file_size_bigint（down_revision=3580c0dcd005，upgrade: alter_column INT → BigInteger NOT NULL），同步更新 01_schema.sql 的 DDL 与 alembic_version seed 到新 head；对现有 ragwebui 已手工 ALTER 并 UPDATE alembic_version，check_schema.sql 输出 file_size = bigint NO（NOT NULL）✓。产物：scripts/ralph/check_schema.sql（打印 7 张表 column_name/data_type/is_nullable 等）、scripts/ralph/expected_schema.md（Python vs Java vs MySQL 对照 + 末尾列出已知遗留 deltas 作为后续 backlog）。verified: ./mvnw -DskipTests package 退出 0；`from app.models.knowledge import Document` 打印 BIGINT / nullable=False；mysql < check_schema.sql 结果与 expected_schema.md 各表一致。

userStories.title: Wire JWT propagation: Gateway → Java services → Feign
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want my token to flow through gateway and downstream feign calls so protected endpoints see my identity.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify rag-common-service/.../config/WebMvcConfig.java registers AuthInterceptor via addInterceptors and excludes /api/ai/auth/** + any @AnonymousAccess paths

rag-common-service/.../config/FeignConfiguration.java provides a feign.RequestInterceptor bean that copies the X-Token / Authorization header from the inbound request (RequestContextHolder) to the outbound feign request
Add a GlobalFilter to rag-gateway: validate JWT presence (skip /api/ai/auth/**), reject with 401 + Result.error if missing/invalid, otherwise let the request pass with the original header intact
Manual verification: `curl -H 'X-Token: <valid>' :8080/knowledge-base` returns 200 with the caller's KBs; `curl :8080/knowledge-base` returns 401
Build/Typecheck passes (cd rag-common-service && ./mvnw -DskipTests package && cd ../rag-gateway && ./mvnw -DskipTests package)

userStories.title: Align embedding model between Java writer and Python reader
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a single embedding model serving both Java doc-processing writes and Python retrieval reads so vector dimensions match and retrieval recalls correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Pick option A or B and document the choice in commit body

Option A: rag-document-service introduces an EmbeddingFactory mirroring Python EmbeddingsFactory (openai/dashscope/ollama), reading provider+model from nacos-config/rag-document-service.yaml; Java config keys must match Python settings keys

Option B: Java stops calling vectorStore.add(); after writing chunks to MySQL it issues a synchronous HTTP POST to rag-ai-service /internal/embed-and-index with {kb_id, chunks[]}; Python receives, embeds, writes to ChromaDB collection kb_{kb_id}
Either way: provider+model is configured in exactly one place per environment
Manual verification: after processing a document via Java, POST /api/ai/knowledge-base/test-retrieval (Python) with a query from that document returns at least one hit with score > 0.5
Build/Typecheck passes (cd rag-document-service && ./mvnw -DskipTests package && python -c "from app.services.embedding.embedding_factory import EmbeddingsFactory")

userStories.title: Build all Java modules in dependency order via mvnw
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every Java module to compile and package locally so I can run them without a system mvn install.
acceptanceCriteria: Run in order from rag-service/: (cd rag-common && ./mvnw clean install -DskipTests) && (cd rag-common-service && ./mvnw clean install -DskipTests) && (cd rag-user-service && ./mvnw clean package -DskipTests) && (cd rag-document-service && ./mvnw clean package -DskipTests) && (cd rag-knowledge-service && ./mvnw clean package -DskipTests) && (cd rag-gateway && ./mvnw clean package -DskipTests)
Each business module produces a runnable Spring Boot fat jar in <module>/target/*.jar
Add scripts/ralph/build-java.sh that runs the chained command above and exits non-zero on any failure
Build/Typecheck passes (bash scripts/ralph/build-java.sh exits 0)

userStories.title: Validate startup order and Nacos service registration
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a single command/checklist to bring up the whole stack and verify all services registered with Nacos before any client traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: Add scripts/ralph/start-all.sh that: (1) verifies docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml ps shows nacos/db/chromadb/minio healthy; (2) starts rag-ai-service in background (uvicorn on 8000); (3) starts each Java service in background using java -jar <module>/target/*.jar; (4) waits for each port to accept connections

Add scripts/ralph/check-nacos.sh that calls Nacos open API GET /nacos/v1/ns/instance/list?serviceName=<name> for rag-ai-service, rag-user-service, rag-knowledge-service, rag-document-service, rag-gateway, and prints a green ✓ for each healthy instance
Manual verification: bash scripts/ralph/check-nacos.sh prints 5 ✓; curl http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/ai/auth/info returns the gateway-routed user-service response (200 with token, 401 without)
Build/Typecheck passes (bash -n scripts/ralph/start-all.sh && bash -n scripts/ralph/check-nacos.sh)

userStories.title: End-to-end RAG smoke test through gateway:8080
userStories.description: As a tester, I want a single script to walk the full pipeline so I can prove the whole RAG flow is working post-microservice-split.
acceptanceCriteria: Add scripts/ralph/e2e-smoke.sh that, against http://127.0.0.1:8080, performs: (1) POST /api/ai/auth/register a fresh user; (2) POST /api/ai/auth/token to capture token; (3) POST /knowledge-base to create a KB and capture kb_id; (4) PUT /knowledge-base/{kb_id}/documents/upload with a sample .md or .txt under fixtures/ to capture upload_id; (5) POST /api/ai/documents/process with the upload_id to capture task_id; (6) poll GET /api/ai/documents/process/{task_id} until status=COMPLETED (timeout 120s); (7) POST /api/ai/chat/... with a question grounded in the document and assert the response references the source
Script exits 0 only when all 7 steps return success
Add fixtures/sample.md with a few paragraphs of distinctive text so the chat answer can be checked
Manual verification: bash scripts/ralph/e2e-smoke.sh exits 0 against a freshly started stack
Build/Typecheck passes (bash -n scripts/ralph/e2e-smoke.sh)

userStories.title: Align frontend API paths to the new gateway
userStories.description: As a frontend user, I want the Next.js app to talk to the new gateway so the UI flow (login → KB → upload → chat) works in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/.env or frontend/.env.development sets NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8080 (or http://127.0.0.1.nip.io if keeping nginx)
Audit all fetch/axios calls in frontend/: KB calls go to /knowledge-base/** (the Java route), not /api/ai/knowledge-base/**
Auth header name used by the frontend matches Java GConstants.JWT_TOKEN_HEADER (X-Token); update frontend interceptor/axios default header accordingly
File upload uses multipart field name 'file' to match Java DocumentController @RequestParam("file") on PUT /knowledge-base/{kb_id}/documents/upload
Manual verification: in a browser at http://localhost:3000 — register, login, create KB, upload a file, ask a question, see answer with citations
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Build/Typecheck passes (cd frontend && npm run build OR npx tsc --noEmit)
